question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered balance file with a negative account balance value, which if not validated by the line parser, gets persisted to the database and causes downstream balance queries to return negative balances, corrupting the financial integrity of the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted balance file that causes the transactionTimeout=5 minutes to be reached exactly when the final batchPersister.persist() call is in progress, causing the transaction to be rolled back after all intermediate batches have been committed, resulting in a partially-persisted balance snapshot?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getFileBufferSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted balance file with a header section that is exactly fileBufferSize=200,000 bytes long, causing the BufferedReader to exhaust its buffer before reaching the data section and triggering an InvalidDatasetException that prevents any balance data from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered balance file with a crafted SHA-384 hash collision in the file content, causing the DigestInputStream in CsvBalanceFileReader.read() to compute an incorrect fileHash that is persisted to the database, allowing the attacker to later submit a different file with the same hash and bypass integrity verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered balance file with a consensusTimestamp in the far future (year 2262), causing the balance parser to accept and persist future-dated balance snapshots that corrupt the temporal ordering of balance history and cause incorrect balance lookups for all accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getBatchSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered balance file with batchSize=200,000 entries all having the same account ID but different token balances, causing the tokenBalances HashMap to silently discard all but the first token balance via putIfAbsent, resulting in incorrect token balance data being persisted without any error?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted balance file that causes the batch.flushInterval=2s timer to fire during a large batch write, causing a concurrent flush that interleaves with the main transaction and produces duplicate or missing balance rows in the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered balance file with a malformed timestamp header that passes the lenient V1 parser (which checks up to MAX_HEADER_ROWS=10 rows) but contains an incorrect timestamp, causing the wrong consensusTimestamp to be associated with all balance entries in the file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getFileBufferSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted balance file with fileBufferSize=200,000 null bytes embedded in account balance lines, causing the UTF-8 decoding in CsvBalanceFileReader to produce garbled account IDs that are persisted to the database as valid balance entries?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered balance file that causes the retry.multiplier=2 exponential backoff to produce a delay exceeding maxBackoff=10s, causing the balance parser to be unavailable for extended periods and creating a window where downstream services serve stale balance data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted balance file with a consensusTimestamp that is 1 nanosecond before the processingTimeout=10s health check boundary, causing the health indicator to report the system as healthy while the balance data is actually stale, masking a processing failure?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getBatchSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered balance file with exactly batchSize*5L=1,000,000 entries (the batch.maxItems limit), causing the batch processor to accept the maximum number of items in a single batch and then fail during the final persist, rolling back all 1,000,000 balance entries and leaving a complete gap in balance history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered balance file with account IDs in shard.realm.num format that contain values exceeding the valid range (e.g., shard=Long.MAX_VALUE), causing the account ID parser to produce an invalid entity ID that is persisted to the database and corrupts account balance lookups?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getFileBufferSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted balance file where each line is exactly fileBufferSize=200,000 characters long, causing the BufferedReader to allocate a 200,000-character internal buffer for each line read, multiplied by batchSize=200,000 lines, exhausting all available heap memory?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted balance file that causes the batch.window=5m batching window to be triggered, causing multiple balance files to be batched together in a single transaction, and then injecting a malformed entry in the last file to roll back all batched files' balance data simultaneously?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getBatchSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered balance file with batchSize=200,000 entries where each entry has the maximum number of token balances, causing the tokenBalances HashMap to grow to batchSize * max_tokens entries before any flush, exhausting heap memory and crashing the importer process?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered balance file with a consensusTimestamp that is identical to the last successfully processed file, causing the parser to attempt to re-process the file and either overwrite existing balance data or fail with a constraint violation that exhausts all retry attempts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted balance file with a malformed version header that causes the CsvBalanceFileReader.supports() check to return false, causing the file to be rejected without any retry, and if this is the only available balance file, causing a permanent gap in balance history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered balance file that causes the @Transactional annotation's 5-minute timeout to be reached during the accountBalanceFile.setCount() and setLoadEnd() calls at the end of doParse(), causing the transaction to roll back after all balance data has been written but before the file metadata is committed, leaving orphaned balance rows?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getBatchSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered balance file with exactly 1 entry (the minimum valid file), causing the batchSize=200,000 threshold to never be reached and the entire file to be processed in the post-loop batchPersister.persist() call, which if it fails silently drops the single balance entry without any retry?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted balance file with a consensusTimestamp that is 1 nanosecond after the endDate configuration boundary, causing the DateRangeFilter to silently skip the entire file without any error, creating a gap in balance history that downstream services cannot distinguish from a legitimate gap?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered balance file with a crafted account balance line that causes the AccountBalanceLineParser.parse() to throw an InvalidDatasetException, which is caught and logged but returns null, causing the entry to be silently dropped and the final count to be incorrect, leading to reconciliation failures?
